channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: The Lecturer and Philosopher King: Xi Jinping Behind Closed Doors
item.description: Encounters with other world leaders reveal a side of China’s leader that the public rarely sees, and offer clues to how he will approach President Trump in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s leader, at the National People’s Congress in Beijing in March.

item.title: Britain Has Had 5 Leaders in a Decade. Is It About to Get Another?
item.description: The promise of Keir Starmer’s Labour Party in 2024 was renewed stability — but that now appears to be under threat.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: Boris Johnson, left, Liz Truss and David Cameron

item.title: Gulf Countries Arrest Shiite ‘Traitors’ Amid War With Iran
item.description: Dozens of Gulf citizens have been accused of belonging to Iran-linked terrorism cells as the war accelerates a shift toward deeper authoritarianism in the region.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke billowing after a drone strike in Kuwait City in March.

item.title: Russia-Ukraine War Shows Cease-Fires Have Lost Meaning Under Trump
item.description: Temporary truces have become a tool of performative diplomacy, an end in themselves rather than a prelude to a lasting settlement, analysts say.
item.dc:creator: Marc Santora
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Explosions over Kyiv, Ukraine, as air defenses shot down a Russian drone on Tuesday.

item.title: Are These the Bones of the Fourth Musketeer? This Dutch Village Hopes So.
item.description: Wolder, near the Belgian border, is waiting to see if the skeleton it dug up in a church is Count d’Artagnan, from Alexandre Dumas’s tale.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.category: Artagnan, Chas de Baatz, Castelmore, D'
item.media:description: An excavation in St. Peter and Paul’s Church in Wolder, the Netherlands, to see if remains found in the church belong to Count d’Artagnan, who was dramatized, fictionalized and immortalized in Dumas’s “The Three Musketeers.”

item.title: Top Gaza Negotiator Urges Hamas to Embrace Rebuilding Plan
item.description: Nickolay Mladenov, who is overseeing the U.S.-led truce in Gaza, has tried to convince the militant group to give up its arms, but it has so far refused amid Israeli cease-fire violations.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Adam Rasgon and Aaron Boxerman
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of Hamas in Gaza City in November.

item.title: How China Sent a Message to Trump on His Arrival
item.description: Beijing welcomed President Trump with a high-ranking vice president, but the choice of a ceremonial leader suggests China is trading symbolism for substance.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo, Pei-Lin Wu and Pablo Robles

item.title: Nigel Farage, Leader of Reform U.K., Faces Investigation for £5 Million Gift
item.description: The Conservative Party referred the case against Mr. Farage, the leader of a right-wing populist party in Britain, to a parliamentary watchdog.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Nigel Farage attending the opening of Parliament in London on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Is in China as Iran War Continues With No End in Sight
item.description: Before leaving Washington on Tuesday, the president reiterated threats to decimate Iran if it doesn’t agree to a deal to resolve the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump departing Joint Base Andrews in Maryland for China on Tuesday.

item.title: A Tech Tycoon’s Prosecution Raises Fears of Authoritarian Overreach
item.description: Nadiem Makarim founded a popular app before joining Indonesia’s government. Now he could face 18 years in prison, targeted in what critics call a dubious anticorruption campaign.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee
item.media:description: Nadiem Makarim, a former education minister and founder of the ride-hailing app Gojek, in court in Jakarta, Indonesia, on Wednesday.

item.title: Chaos in Philippines Senate as Duterte Ally Faces Arrest
item.description: The sound of gunshots, apparently from inside the chamber, was broadcast on live television as a senator in the Philippines, who was an ally of the former leader Rodrigo Duterte, faced arrest.
item.dc:creator: Jason Gutierrez
item.media:credit: Jam Sta Rosa/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Police personnel arrive as people react to the gunshots heard inside the Senate of the Philippines in Manila on Monday.

item.title: Catherine, Princess of Wales, to Make First Official Trip Abroad Since Cancer Diagnosis
item.description: Catherine is visiting Reggio Emilia, a city celebrated for its approach to early childhood education, a deep interest of hers. It is her first official visit abroad since her cancer diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo and Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:description: Catherine, Princess of Wales, arriving at the town hall in Reggio Emilia, Italy, on Wednesday.

item.title: Beware of Drunk Deer, French Police Say, Announcing Season of Inebriation
item.description: Some wild animals, eating fermented or rotten fruits, “may exhibit completely unpredictable behavior,” the police warned drivers in Saône-et-Loire, a rural region in central-eastern France.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

item.title: Why A.I. is the Hidden Minefield of Trump’s China Visit
item.description: The leaders of both countries are expected to discuss the risks from artificial intelligence, but neither country is willing to be the first to slow down.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping meeting in Busan, South Korea, in 2025.

item.title: Xi referenced ‘great changes unseen in a century.’ Here’s what that means.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, said on Thursday that the world was undergoing a “once-in-a-century transformation.”

item.title: Xi Says U.S.-China Relations Could Turn ‘Dangerous’ Over Taiwan
item.description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping said relations between the two countries could enter an “extremely dangerous place” if Mr. Trump ignored China’s demands over Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: Patriot missile systems on display during a Taiwanese military exercise in Taipei last year.

item.title: Inside Trump’s Summit Tour in Beijing: From Temple of Heaven to the Great Hall
item.description: The Temple of Heaven and Great Hall of the People embody distinct parts of China’s history, which Xi Jinping has sought to underscore while hosting state visits.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Joy Dong
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: Mr. Trump and Mr. Xi at the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Trump’s 2017 visit to Beijing offers clues for this year’s summit.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: President Trump, Xi Jinping and their wives at a Chinese opera in Beijing’s Forbidden City in 2017.

item.title: President Trump Goes to China
item.description: His talks with Xi Jinping in Beijing could decide whether the United States and China maintain their uneasy truce on trade.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, visiting the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Beijing Tightens Security As Trump and Xi Meet in China
item.description: Historic sites and roads have been closed in the Chinese capital for President Trump’s state visit, resulting in severe traffic and disappointed tourists.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and Murphy Zhao

item.title: Judge Orders U.S. to Return Colombian Woman Deported to Congo
item.description: The unusual ruling came after the judge found that the Trump administration had most likely violated the law by deporting the 55-year-old woman to the African country despite its refusal to take her.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:description: The Colombian woman and other migrants are housed in a hotel outside Kinshasa, the capital of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Why Does China Want Taiwan?
item.description: There are few issues in diplomacy more complicated than the status of the self-governing island, which China claims as its own. It is almost certain to come up when President Trump meets China’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
item.media:description: Taiwan is certain to be raised by China’s leader, Xi Jinping, when he hosts President Trump this week.

item.title: Activists, Lawmakers Urge Trump to Call for Release of Jimmy Lai and Dong Yuyu
item.description: Supporters of Mr. Lai, a Hong Kong dissident, and Mr. Dong, a Chinese journalist, hope President Trump will raise their cases with President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jimmy Lai at his home in Hong Kong in 2020. He was sentenced in February to 20 years in prison after Beijing blamed the Hong Kong publisher for leading the city’s major protests nearly seven years ago.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo and David E. Sanger

item.title: Canada and Alberta Reach Agreement to Raise Carbon Prices as Pipeline Tradeoff
item.description: But the agreement is the latest softening of carbon reduction measures introduced by Prime Minister Mark Carney since he took office last year.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Pat Kane for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Syncrude facility near Fort McMurray, Alberta, producing synthetic crude oil from oil sands.

item.title: The China Gambit: From Nixon to Trump
item.description: President Trump’s visit to Beijing comes as relations with China have become more combative.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:description: President Richard M. Nixon visiting Beijing in 1972.

item.title: As Trump and Xi Meet in China, American and Chinese People Are Drifting Apart
item.description: Past meetings between the presidents of the U.S. and China have involved friendly displays of cultural exchange. Those gestures have largely dried up.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:description: President Trump and the first lady, Melania Trump, with the Chinese leader, Xi Jinping, and his wife, Peng Liyuan, during a tour of the Forbidden City, in Beijing in 2017.

item.title: Black Bear Fatally Mauls Uranium Contractor in Northern Canada
item.description: The attack, at a remote uranium mining site in northern Saskatchewan, was only the fourth fatal black bear encounter in the province’s recorded history, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:description: The black bear is the most common species in Canada, though bear attacks are rare.

item.title: Why Xi Doesn’t Need a Deal With Trump
item.description: President Xi Jinping of China is buying time for Beijing and may see an opening with a U.S. president weakened by the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: President Xi Jinping of China, seen here in Beijing on Tuesday, has been seeking to redefine the terms of how China and the United States should engage.

item.title: Alberta Separation Petition Is Dealt a Court Blow
item.description: A judge sided with an Indigenous group that had asked for a petition by separatists to force a vote on the oil-rich province seceding from Canada to be thrown out.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Separatist leader Mitch Sylvestre is seen during a rally in front of the Elections Alberta headquarters in Edmonton, Canada, this month, as they submit boxes of signatures in the hope of triggering an independence referendum.

item.title: Trump in Beijing
item.description: The president is getting the red carpet treatment, but the deep rift between the U.S. and China will be very hard to repair.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: At the airport in Beijing for President Trump’s arrival.

item.title: Cynthia Shange, Who Defied Apartheid at a Beauty Pageant, Dies at 76
item.description: She was the first Black South African to enter the Miss World contest, placing in the top five in 1972.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.media:description: Cynthia Shange competing at a Miss World event in London in 1972. South Africa’s president praised the boldness of her ambitions “in an era when apartheid segregation was at its peak.”

item.title: Israel Says Netanyahu Made a Secret Trip to U.A.E., Which Says He Didn’t
item.description: The claim and the denial signaled both how close Israel and the United Arab Emirates have become, and how delicate that relationship remains.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem in April.

item.title: More U.S. Spy Planes are Surveilling Cuba
item.description: As President Trump threatens to act against Cuba, experts are debating whether the flights, which the United States is not taking pains to hide, are just intimidation or foreshadow military force.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles, Eric Schmitt and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A taxi driver in Havana earlier this month.

item.title: What to Watch at Trump and Xi’s U.S.-China Summit
item.description: Here’s what to know about the biggest sources of tension in U.S.-China relations before the first summit in Beijing in nine years between the nations’ leaders.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport, Ana Swanson and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Eric Trump, Who Runs the Family Business, Hops Along on the Plane to China
item.description: The president’s middle son is said to be joining his father in a “personal capacity” rather than to make business deals.
item.dc:creator: Ben Protess
item.media:description: President Trump and his delegation, including his son Eric Trump, at the Beijing Capital Airport on Wednesday.

item.title: Chinese Firms Plot Secret Arms Sales to Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The effort involves plans to send weapons through other countries in an effort to hide the origins of the shipments.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Mark Mazzetti and Dustin Volz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes diplomatic visit with President Xi Jinping of China.

item.title: For Trump, the Iran War Looms Over China Summit
item.description: The balance of power between the United States and China had shifted in Beijing’s favor even before the war in Iran began in February.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump leaving the White House on Tuesday. He arrived in Beijing today.

item.title: Why the Bombing of Iran Tied the U.S. More Closely to China
item.description: As the U.S. tries to rebuild its weapons stockpiles drained in the Iran war, it will need access to rare-earth minerals, an industry China dominates.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A destroyed building in Tehran last month. The war in Iran is expected to figure into talks between the United States and China in Beijing this week.

item.title: France Clears Cruise Ship to Sail After Norovirus Outbreak Sickens Dozens
item.description: The ship’s 1,700 passengers had been prevented from disembarking to Bordeaux for hours while officials investigated a wave of illness on board.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:description: Passengers aboard the Ambition, a ship operated by Ambassador Cruise Line, have been prevented from leaving the vessel while it is docked at the Bordeaux port in France.

item.title: Trump and Xi: Beneath the Pomp and Niceties, a Geopolitical Rivalry
item.description: The relationship between the two leaders is marked just as much by mistrust and confrontation as it is by niceties.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping of China after a meeting in Busan, South Korea, last year. They are set to meet again this week.

item.title: Fuel Prices Drive Sales of E.V.s, Just Not in the U.S.
item.description: Electric vehicle sales have soared in Europe and much of the rest of the world, but Americans are still hesitant.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: High fuel prices are weighing on sales of vehicles that run on gasoline or diesel.

item.title: A Physicist Who Thinks in Poetry from the Cosmic Edge
item.description: In her second pop-science book, theoretical cosmologist Chanda Prescod-Weinstein returns to her celestial and cultural roots.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller
item.category: The Disordered Cosmos: A Journey into Dark Matter, Spacetime, and Dreams Deferred (Book)
item.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times

item.title: French Hantavirus Patient Is Critically Ill as Outbreak Reaches 11 Cases
item.description: The woman, who was a passenger on the MV Hondius, was breathing with the help of an artificial lung, officials in Paris said.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands and Ségolène Le Stradic
item.media:description: Passengers after disembarking from the MV Hondius on Monday.

item.title: King’s Speech Comes at an Awkward Time for Starmer
item.description: King Charles III read out Keir Starmer’s legislative agenda in the traditional manner, even as the British prime minister’s leadership remained under pressure.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: King Charles III sat beside Queen Camilla as he spoke during the State Opening of Parliament in London on Wednesday.

item.title: As Starmer Rejects Calls to Resign, Some Make Comparisons to President Biden
item.description: In Britain, some argue that Prime Minister Keir Starmer, by rejecting calls to step aside, risks repeating the mistakes of President Joseph R. Biden Jr.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: For Britain’s prime minister, Keir Starmer, the parallels with former President Joseph R. Biden Jr. may not be welcome, but they are certainly evident.

item.title: What to Know About the Federal Gas Tax Trump Wants to Suspend
item.description: President Trump said he would like to suspend the 18.4-cent-a-gallon tax, but it’s a move that may save drivers only a few dollars a month.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have climbed more than 50 percent since February.

item.title: Trump Tower Deal in Australia Falls Apart And a Blame Game Begins
item.description: The property developer says the war in Iran and Trump’s “unpopularity” contributed to the collapse of a plan for a 91-story building on the Gold Coast.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung
item.media:credit: Matthew Abbott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Surfers Paradise in Australia’s Gold Coast, where the proposed Trump Tower would be.

item.title: Europe Tries a Trumpian Tactic With Trump: No Apologies
item.description: Stuck with the fallout from America’s war in Iran, European leaders have criticized the president publicly. When he’s been angered, they haven’t backed down.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany is one of several European leaders who has openly criticized President Trump.

item.title: Trump’s Shrinking Ambitions on China
item.description: The president came into office planning harsher trade moves on China than on the rest of the world. Here’s why he’s had to scale them back.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: When President Trump tried to force China into making concessions last year by threatening extreme tariffs, the tactic backfired, forcing the United States to pare back its goals.

item.title: How China Could Wield Its Control of Rare Earths Against Trump
item.description: A central question hanging over the summit this week is whether China will agree to extend a temporary postponement of even tougher rare-earth export controls.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: Steel mills, chemical factories and rare-earth refineries in Baotou, China. The Trump administration said it was in frequent communication with China about rare earths.

item.title: U.S. and Mexican Officials Deny C.I.A. Had Lethal Role in Mexico Operation
item.description: The denials came after a CNN report claimed the C.I.A. had been involved in the assassination of a cartel member inside Mexico earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Maria Abi-Habib
item.media:credit: Carl De Souza/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: “The Government of Mexico categorically rejects any version that seeks to normalize, justify, or suggest the existence of lethal, covert, or unilateral operations by foreign agencies on national territory,” said Omar García Harfuch, Mexico’s security secretary

item.title: Dominican Republic Agrees to Take Third-Country Migrants Deported by U.S.
item.description: The agreement, which reverses President Luis Abinader’s previous stance, is part of President Trump’s efforts to find governments willing to accept deportees.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and Hogla Enecia Pérez
item.media:description: President Luis Abinader of the Dominican Republic previously said, “We do not have, nor will we accept, nor are we obligated to, nor will we accept receiving people from other countries.”

item.title: Battle Over the Strait of Hormuz Leaves Safe Passage a Gamble
item.description: Vessels are traveling without transmitting critical information meant to keep the waters safe, maritime intelligence experts say.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman on Friday.

item.title: Israeli Report Finds That Sexual Violence by Hamas Was Widespread
item.description: A two-year investigation by a team of researchers in Israel concluded that sexual violence by Hamas and its allies was widespread during and after the Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: A house in kibbutz Kfar Aza, Israel, that was among those attacked on Oct. 7, 2023.

item.title: Earthquake Shakes Tehran, Where Nerves Are Already Strained by Iran War
item.description: Iranian media said a moderate quake had shaken the capital around midnight local time.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Shows Iran Retains Substantial Missile Capabilities
item.description: Secret new assessments say Iran has operational access to 30 of its 33 missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting that its military remains far stronger than President Trump has asserted.
item.dc:creator: Adam Entous, Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Missile displays in Tehran in 2024. According to classified U.S. intelligence assessments, Iran retains roughly 70 percent of its prewar missile stockpile.
